artPrompt: A determined human organizer woman portrait, sleeves rolled, gripping a wooden clipboard with one fist raised mid-rally, a pitchfork slung over her shoulder and petunias tucked in her collar, an out-of-focus crowd of townsfolk seething behind her in the gloom. One warm low sun catches her jaw and the curl of her hair, the rest dropping into brooding shadow. Rendered as a moody oil painting — old-master chiaroscuro, visible impasto brushwork, deep shadow, warm varnished tones.
name: Petunia Pitchfork, Recall Organizer
text:
When Petunia Pitchfork enters the battlefield, hold a town hall: each player creates a 1/1 green and white Voter creature token. You create one extra, because you brought a clipboard.
Whenever you cast a spell or one or more Voters enter under your control, put a signature counter on Petunia Pitchfork.
Grassroots — Whenever the table votes, you may have each Voter you control cast an extra vote. (Yes, that's legal here. Petunia checked.)
{2}{G}{W}, Remove three signature counters from among permanents you control: Recall the incumbent. Each opponent who controls more permanents than you sacrifices a permanent of your choice. It's not personal. It's democracy.
flavorText: "We're not a mob. We're an organized mob. There's a sign-up sheet."

extras.frontrunnerPunish: recall whoever has the most permanents
